memories.memory_text: I called out to Nick from the kitchen, telling him breakfast was ready, but he didn’t answer—just kept on with that water-pick like I wasn’t even there. When he finally came in, he sat down and started eating without looking at me.
grounded_facts: Janine called out to Tudor that breakfast was ready.
Tudor did not respond and continued using the water-pick.
Tudor entered the dining room and began eating without speaking or making eye contact.

memories.memory_text: I asked if I could call him at the office, and he just stared at his food, asking why I’d want to. When he said he’d be out all day checking garages, I didn’t say anything—I just pushed my food around my plate. It felt like even wanting to reach out was wrong.
grounded_facts: Janine asked Tudor if she could call him at the office.
Tudor questioned her reason and stated he would not be at the office much.
Janine remained silent and fidgeted with her food after his response.
Tudor did not engage with her emotionally and continued eating.

memories.memory_text: I heard a loud splash and some strange noise from the bathroom, so I poked my head around the corner—Nick was bent over, gripping his stomach like something was really wrong.
grounded_facts: Janine heard disturbances from the bathroom (water splashing
unusual sounds)
She physically checked in by poking her head around the corner
She observed Tudor in a pained posture
which prompted her to ask if he said something

memories.memory_text: I asked if he was okay, but he didn’t answer—just kept brushing his teeth like I wasn’t even there, and after a while I sighed and walked away, feeling like he didn’t want me close.
grounded_facts: Janine asked Tudor if he was okay
Tudor did not respond and continued using the water-pick
She sighed conspicuously before leaving
She perceived his silence as rejection

memories.memory_text: I remember sitting on the sauna bench, the heat pressing down as Betts leaned in and told me to get a doctor to see Nick without him knowing—her voice low and sure, like we were sharing a secret I wasn’t sure I wanted to keep. I just whispered, 'He'll be really mad,' feeling my chest tighten, but part of me wanted to do it, to finally know.
grounded_facts: Janine is in the sauna with Betts and Benda, wrapped in a towel.
Betts suggests that Janine contact Dr. St. Luc about Nick's lumps and arrange a visit without telling Nick.
Janine responds with concern that Nick will be angry, indicating internal conflict.
Janine does not reject the idea, showing some level of interest or willingness.

memories.scene_title: 17、INT. CLINIC RECEPTION AT STARLINER TOWERS -- AFTERNOON
memories.memory_text: I said 'Excuse me, Brad. Gotta go,' when Dr. St. Luc called my name—my voice sounded shaky even to me, and I could feel my hands trembling as I stood up. I didn’t look back at Mr. Parkins, just followed the doctor down the hall, my stomach tight with worry about what he might say about Tudor’s condition.
grounded_facts: Janine was waiting in the clinic reception with other patients, including Mr. Parkins.
Mr. Parkins was talking to her about mega-vitamins and aging when she was called in.
Janine spoke the words 'Excuse me, Brad. Gotta go' as she left for the consultation.
She followed Dr. St. Luc into his office, and he closed the door behind them.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying 'That's great, Doctor. Thanks,' after he finally agreed to come see Nick tonight—my hands were shaking a little, but I managed to smile and leave without looking back.
grounded_facts: Janine asked Dr. St. Luc to come see her husband at home.
Dr. St. Luc agreed to visit at around 9:30 or 10:00 p.m.
Janine expressed gratitude and left the office after the conversation.
Janine smiled and shook her head in response to the timing, indicating it was acceptable.

memories.memory_text: I picked up a few things from the shelves, but the thought of cooking or even eating made my stomach turn—I couldn't do it, so I just left with the magazine.
grounded_facts: Janine looked at several shelves of food and picked up various items.
She felt repulsed by the idea of cooking or eating.
She ultimately did not purchase any food, only a Vogue magazine.

memories.memory_text: I walked down the hallway, knocked gently on Betts's door, and let myself in—so quiet, so familiar, like I’ve done it a hundred times before.
grounded_facts: Janine walks along a hallway.
Janine stops at a door and knocks gently.
Janine opens the door and walks in.
Janine is obviously familiar with the occupant.

memories.memory_text: Betts looked up from her photos and told me to come back for dinner after Dr. St. Luc left, saying she’d be lonely if I didn’t — her voice was warm, insistent, like she really wanted me there.
grounded_facts: Betts is sitting on the floor surrounded by large black-and-white photographs and work materials, indicating her involvement in advertising or graphic design.
Betts mentions ordering French food for two and explicitly invites Janine to return for company and supper after Dr. St. Luc’s visit.
Janine does not speak but nods in response and later wiggles her fingers in a quiet goodbye.

memories.memory_text: I kept flipping through my Vogue, but I couldn’t focus—nothing held my attention, not even the clothes. I turned the TV on and cranked the volume up, just to fill the silence while I waited for St. Luc.
grounded_facts: Janine is sitting on the couch in Tudor's apartment, flipping through a Vogue magazine.
She is wearing large, fashionable glasses with thick, tinted prescription lenses.
She is unable to concentrate on any activity until St. Luc arrives.
She turns on the TV and increases the volume to an uncomfortable level.

memories.memory_text: I was so tired, just waking up, and Nick’s voice kept calling me in that strange, wailing way—I couldn’t ignore it. When I sat beside him, he held me too tight and suddenly asked if I wanted to make love, saying I was the most sexy thing alive, over and over, like he wasn’t really seeing me.
grounded_facts: Janine was dozing on the couch and woke up when Tudor called her repeatedly.
She walked into the bedroom and could not see clearly without her glasses or contacts.
Tudor was sitting on the bed, pale but smiling ecstatically, and put his arm around her.
He made an insistent, emotionally charged sexual advance, repeating 'Do you want to make love?' and calling her 'the most sexy thing alive.'
Janine reacted with stiffness and visible discomfort.

memories.memory_text: I saw a black thread in the corner of Nick's mouth—like an insect's leg—and when I reached to brush it away, it twitched and pulled back inside. I yanked my hand away, covering my mouth, too terrified to breathe.
grounded_facts: Janine notices a black thread-like object in Tudor's mouth.
She reaches toward it, and the object twitches back into Tudor's mouth upon contact.
She reacts by jerking back and covering her mouth with her fists.

memories.memory_text: The thing came out more—Nick's lips parting like it was alive—and I couldn't stay. I don't remember getting up, but I was running, heart pounding, needing to get out before that thing fully emerged.
grounded_facts: The parasite begins to emerge further as Tudor's lips part.
Janine flees the room in panic after witnessing the ongoing emergence.
Her flight follows the moment of realization that something is coming out of Tudor's body.

memories.memory_text: I ran down the hallway to Betts's apartment, crying so hard I could barely see, my breath catching between sobs, and I didn’t stop—I just opened the door and went in.
grounded_facts: Janine is running down a hallway leading to Betts's apartment
Janine is sobbing and stumbling while moving
Janine opens Betts's door without hesitation

memories.memory_text: I called out for Betts, my voice trembling, and then saw her on the balcony—so still, her face painted in a way I didn’t recognize. When she turned and opened her arms, I froze for a second, unsure if this was really her.
grounded_facts: Janine entered Betts' apartment and looked around for her.
Janine called out 'Betts? Betts? It's me.'
Janine saw Betts on the balcony wearing extreme makeup that surprised her.
Betts turned slowly, smiled, and opened her arms.

memories.memory_text: I was sobbing in Betts's lap, my face pressed against her thighs, and she was rocking me like I was a child, stroking my hair—her voice so calm, telling me not to worry, that everything was in the genes, and I felt so small, so soothed, whispering that she felt cosy, so cosy, like safety.
grounded_facts: Janine has her head in Betts's lap on the sofa
Janine is sobbing while expressing existential fear
Betts rocks Janine and strokes her hair
Janine says Betts feels 'very good' and has a 'cosy body'
Janine is visibly soothed despite continued sobbing

memories.memory_text: Betts kissed me—soft at first, then her mouth wrenched open, her throat swelling grotesquely, and something alive forcing its way into my mouth; I gagged, tried to pull back, but her hands held me—cold, slick terror as it slithered down my throat.
grounded_facts: Betts initiates a kiss after asking Janine to make love
The kiss turns forceful with Betts opening her mouth wide
Betts's throat swells visibly
A parasite emerges and enters Janine's mouth
Janine gags and struggles briefly before the parasite enters her throat

memories.memory_text: I came up from the water, smiling—I felt so calm, like everything was finally right, and the others were there, watching me, waiting.
grounded_facts: Janine surfaces from the pool smiling radiantly.
The pool environment is dim and tranquil when she emerges.
Other residents, including Betts, are present and calm in the water.
St. Luc is at the pool's edge, observing.

memories.memory_text: I kissed St. Luc under the water, holding his head tight—my throat swelled and something moved up from inside me, forcing its way into him, and I could feel it connecting us like iron.
grounded_facts: Janine kisses St. Luc underwater while he is held by Betts.
Her throat ripples and swells as the parasite moves from her body into his.
Tentacles are visible between her mouth and his during the transfer.
She and St. Luc remain physically connected and sink beneath the surface together.

memories.memory_text: I got two calls from Hobbes out of the blue—one to set up lunch, and then another to change the time. I didn’t think much of it then, but now it’s the only reason I was here.
grounded_facts: St. Luc received unexpected phone calls from Dr. Hobbes to arrange a meeting.
The meeting was scheduled for lunch at Annabelle's apartment.
St. Luc was present at the apartment because of this arrangement.

memories.scene_title: 17、INT. CLINIC RECEPTION AT STARLINER TOWERS -- AFTERNOON
memories.memory_text: I gestured for Janine Tudor to follow me into my office, bypassing the others in the waiting room, including Mr. Parkins who was mid-sentence with her.
grounded_facts: St. Luc gestured to Janine Tudor to follow him into his office.
Janine was sitting in the waiting room among other patients, including Mr. Parkins.
Mr. Parkins was speaking to Janine when St. Luc called her in.
St. Luc closed the door after Janine entered his office.

memories.memory_text: I looked at Tudor's file again after Janine left, something in the numbers didn't add up—the cholesterol was low, blood pressure only slightly elevated, no signs of malignancy last year. I couldn't justify cancer progressing that fast, not like she described.
grounded_facts: St. Luc reviewed Tudor's file after Janine's visit.
He noted normal cholesterol and slightly high blood pressure from a prior check-up.
He expressed doubt about the rapid development of cancer as described by Janine.
He agreed to make a house call, scheduling it for 9:30 or 10:00 p.m.

memories.memory_text: I asked Mr. Parkins if the young woman he had contact with was from Starliner Towers, and he confirmed she lived in unit 1208—my mind immediately went to the cluster of unexplained abdominal growths I’d seen in other patients from that building.
grounded_facts: St. Luc asked Parkins if the girl was from Starliner Towers
Parkins confirmed she lived in 1208 at Starliner Towers
St. Luc is aware of other similar cases from that location

memories.memory_text: When I asked if her name was Annabelle Horsefield, and he said 'That's the one,' it hit me—she might be the common source, the first case I’ve been trying to trace through these patients with the strange abdominal lumps.
grounded_facts: St. Luc asked if the woman’s name was Annabelle Horsefield
Parkins confirmed her identity
St. Luc had prior knowledge of Annabelle in connection with other patients

memories.memory_text: I was told I'm supposed to see Nick at ten, but I haven't arrived yet—Janine just passed on the message while I was still at Betts' apartment, surrounded by her photos and cluttered equipment on the walls.
grounded_facts: Dr. St. Luc is scheduled to visit Nick at around ten.
Janine informed Betts about Dr. St. Luc's upcoming visit.
The scene takes place at Betts' apartment, which contains advertising and graphic equipment.
Dr. St. Luc is not physically present in the scene; his visit is anticipated.

memories.memory_text: Forsythe dropped a stack of medical files and journals on my desk—Hobbes, Linsky, the Bulletin of the Canadian Medical Association—stuff I’d asked for. I remember saying, 'That's great, Forsythe, great. Thanks,' while already flipping through the papers.
grounded_facts: Forsythe delivered requested files on patients Horsefield, Tudor, Swinburne, and Velakofsky.
She included published papers by Hobbes, Linsky, and Lefebvre from the Bulletin of the Canadian Medical Association and the Journal of the American Medical Association.
St. Luc acknowledged receipt with 'That's great, Forsythe, great. Thanks.'

memories.memory_text: While I was on the phone with Rollo Linsky, I could see Forsythe changing out of her nurse’s uniform in the corner—taking her time, not hiding it but not performing either. I kept one eye on her, one on Hobbes’s notes, while talking.
grounded_facts: Forsythe began undressing and redressing in street clothes near a metal locker during St. Luc's phone call with Rollo Linsky.
She did so without modesty or overt display, in full view of St. Luc.
St. Luc remained on the call while observing her, with Hobbes’s notes also present on screen.

memories.memory_text: I didn't understand what Rollo meant when he said the parasite was 'a disease to save man from his mind'—it made no sense to me at first.
grounded_facts: St Luc expressed confusion in response to Rollo's statement about the parasite.
Rollo began to clarify his explanation after St Luc voiced his confusion.
The statement 'a disease to save man from his mind' was directly quoted by Rollo in the lab.

memories.memory_text: I was holding the Velakofsky file, looking at the X-rays of those dark, blurred masses in his abdomen, when I realized Forsythe was getting dressed—she was leaving—and I told Rollo that Hobbes might not have known, but Annabelle had been with several men at Starliner Towers, and now three or four of them had these unidentifiable growths.
grounded_facts: St. Luc was holding the Velakofsky file containing abdominal X-rays showing dark, blurred masses.
Forsythe was present in the office and was halfway through putting on her street clothes.
St. Luc spoke to Rollo over the phone, stating that three or possibly four men at Starliner Towers had large, free-moving, unidentified abdominal growths.
St. Luc connected the cases to Annabelle's presence at Starliner Towers and implied she may have been involved with the affected men.

memories.memory_text: I agreed to Forsythe’s supper invitation, but only if it was late—after I met Rollo at Tudor’s. I needed to make it clear my work came first.
grounded_facts: St. Luc is in his office at night and has just finished a phone call with Rollo, arranging to meet at apartment 1009, South Tower, Starliner Towers at ten.
Forsythe invites St. Luc to her place for a late supper while he is still at work.
St. Luc initially hesitates, responding 'No. I don't think so,' before agreeing with the condition 'OK. But late.'
After the exchange, Forsythe leaves, and St. Luc returns to his files.

memories.memory_text: Forsythe burst into my office, sobbing and barely able to speak—she’d been attacked, she said, by someone she knew, and she thought she’d killed him.
grounded_facts: Forsythe entered St. Luc's office suddenly, in a state of distress.
She reported being attacked by a man she recognized, who grabbed her and tried to kiss her.
She stated she stabbed him and believed he had fallen, possibly dead.

memories.memory_text: After holding her for a moment, I had to pull back and ask where the man was—I needed to see if he was still there, if this was really about the organism.
grounded_facts: St. Luc hugged Forsythe briefly, then separated from her to obtain information.
He asked about the attacker’s location and expressed urgency to investigate the scene.
He explicitly linked the incident to a possible parasitic organism they suspected.

memories.memory_text: I saw the burn on Mrs. Spergazzi's forearm right away—blistered, raw tissue from that creature's solvent. I told Forsythe to take them back to 703 and treat it as best she could for now, second-degree, no delay.
grounded_facts: St. Luc observed the burn on Mrs. Spergazzi's forearm caused by the parasite's animal-tissue solvent.
He instructed Forsythe to treat Mrs. Spergazzi for second-degree burns in apartment 703.
He emphasized not leaving the apartment and only opening the door for him.

memories.memory_text: After handing my bag to Forsythe, I asked where the creature went—Spergazzi said he threw it down to the incinerator on his cane. I told Forsythe I was going down there, and then I left.
grounded_facts: St. Luc asked Mr. Spergazzi about the location of the parasite after the attack.
Mr. Spergazzi stated he struck the creature with his cane and disposed of it in the incinerator.
St. Luc decided to go to the incinerator and informed Forsythe of his intent before leaving.

memories.memory_text: I couldn't see inside the incinerator at first, so I looked around and spotted the superintendent's flashlight on top of a heating pipe—I took it down, turned it on, and went back to searching for the dead parasite.
grounded_facts: St. Luc enters the incinerator room and attempts to probe inside the oven with a poker but has limited visibility.
He notices the superintendent's flashlight placed on top of a heating pipe.
He retrieves the flashlight, turns it on, and uses it to continue his search.

memories.memory_text: I found the parasite corpse in the incinerator, pulled it out with the poker, and held it up to the light—its insides glowed, twisted and alien, like something wasn't right about its structure.
grounded_facts: St. Luc used a poker to retrieve the parasite corpse from inside the incinerator.
He examined it under a naked light bulb, observing its translucent body and internal anatomy.

memories.memory_text: A man came up behind me, grabbed me, and tried to kiss my neck—then my mouth—I fought back with the poker, hit him in the chest and ankle, and ended up kicking him in the head until he stopped moving.
grounded_facts: An unknown man entered the room, approached St. Luc from behind, and initiated physical contact by kissing his neck.
The man attempted to kiss him on the mouth and tried to pin him down during a struggle.
St. Luc used the poker as a weapon in self-defense, struck the man multiple times, and ultimately stopped after the man lay motionless.

memories.memory_text: I left the body near the incinerator door, blood pooling on the floor—I didn’t have time to move it further before Forsythe came down.
grounded_facts: St. Luc killed a man in the incinerator room prior to Forsythe's arrival.
The body was positioned such that Forsythe stumbled upon it immediately upon entering the space.
There was blood on the floor from the man St. Luc killed.

memories.memory_text: I buzzed the Spergazzi apartment and asked to speak to the nurse, but Mr. Spergazzi said she had gone away—maybe to look for me—and in that moment, I knew something was wrong enough to act.
grounded_facts: St. Luc attempted to contact the Spergazzi apartment via intercom.
Mr. Spergazzi responded that the nurse had left the apartment.
St. Luc reacted by cursing under his breath and moving toward the stairwell.
St. Luc proceeded down the stairs toward the incinerator level after this exchange.

memories.memory_text: I saw bloodied handprints on the door to the garage as soon as I got down the stairs—someone had pressed hard, fingers smeared in panic.
grounded_facts: St. Luc noticed bloodied handprints on the door leading to the garage upon entering the incinerator room.
The handprints were visible immediately upon arrival and were on the door to the garage.

memories.memory_text: When I opened the garage door, Forsythe's screams came echoing out—sharp, desperate, and unmistakably hers.
grounded_facts: St. Luc opened the garage door after noticing the bloodied handprints.
Forsythe's screams were heard immediately after the garage door was opened.

memories.memory_text: I smashed the doorman with his own gun, then fired three times into his upper body—he was covered in blood and drool, and I couldn't risk him hurting Forsythe any longer.
grounded_facts: St. Luc used the doorman's gun to strike him and then shot him three times in the upper body.
The doorman was on top of Forsythe, partially turned toward St. Luc after hitting him in the temple.
St. Luc acted despite the risk of hitting Forsythe, driven by repulsion and urgency.

memories.memory_text: I found Forsythe in her car with the doorman on top of her. I took his gun and hit him with it, then shot him three times. I pulled him off her, got her into the passenger seat, and tried to drive out. A car blocked the exit, so I swerved and hit a post. I got out, fired the gun at the residents, one fell back and blocked the others, then I carried Forsythe out.
grounded_facts: St. Luc found Forsythe in her car with the doorman on top of her.
He took the doorman's gun and used it to strike him before shooting him three times.
He removed the doorman from Forsythe and placed her in the passenger seat.
He attempted to drive out but was blocked by another car, swerved, and damaged the fender on a concrete post.
He opened fire on the emerging residents, one fell back into the car blocking the others.
He carried Forsythe out of the garage toward the incinerator door.

memories.memory_text: I half-dragged Forsythe down the hallway, moving past open doors where people were tangled together on floors and furniture, the air thick with moans and whispers—I just kept my head down and pushed forward.
grounded_facts: St. Luc is physically carrying or supporting Forsythe through the hallway
The hallway has open apartment doors with visible people engaged in sexual acts
There is a continuous background of sexual sounds including moans, giggles, and whispers
St. Luc is moving through the space with effort and purpose

memories.memory_text: I saw the exit door standing open ahead and rushed through it, pulling Forsythe with me—finally out of that noise, out of those rooms.
grounded_facts: St. Luc locates an open exit door at the end of the hallway
St. Luc actively moves through the exit door while carrying Forsythe
The exit represents a transition out of the chaotic environment

memories.memory_text: I checked the gym carefully before pulling Forsythe inside, then blocked the door with a box horse and a weight-lifting table so no one could get in easily.
grounded_facts: St. Luc looks into the gym cautiously before entering
St. Luc pulls Forsythe into the gym
St. Luc barricades the door using a box horse and a weight-lifting table

memories.memory_text: I knelt beside Forsythe as she started to come around, brushed the blood-matted hair from her face, and said Rollo would be here soon—more to steady myself than to reassure her.
grounded_facts: St. Luc kneels beside Forsythe as she regains consciousness
St. Luc strokes her face and pushes blood-matted hair from her eyes
St. Luc says 'Rollo'll be here soon' quietly, seemingly to himself

memories.memory_text: I was about to kiss her—she pulled me in close, whispering that strange dream about Freud, her voice so slow and steady it made my thoughts blur. Her lips were almost on mine when her mouth snapped open and I saw the tentacles moving inside.
grounded_facts: Forsythe drew St. Luc toward her and began speaking in a dreamy

hypnotic manner. St. Luc was close enough to kiss her before noticing the physical change in her mouth. The parasite tentacles became visible as her throat swelled and her mouth opened unnaturally wide.

memories.memory_text: I shoved a wad of cloth into her mouth and tied it shut with a strip from her blouse—she thrashed against me, moaning through the gag. Then I threw her over my shoulder and carried her out, her body writhing the whole way.
grounded_facts: St. Luc created a gag from Forsythe's blouse and secured it to prevent her from removing it. He physically restrained her by pinning her hands and carrying her over his shoulder. Forsythe struggled
moaned
and howled while being carried.

memories.memory_text: I held Forsythe against the wall while blood poured from her mouth and something tore its way out through the gag—tentacles writhing, searching for grip. I let go, and she just sank to the floor.
grounded_facts: St. Luc physically supported Forsythe against the stairway wall
he observed blood soaking through her gag and tentacles emerging from her mouth
he released her
and she collapsed to the floor
this occurred while residents surrounded them
moaning and making contact

memories.memory_text: From above, I looked down and saw a resident pull the thing from her mouth and swallow it whole. Others were touching her body—stroking, caressing—like it was some kind of ritual. I couldn't stay. I ran.
grounded_facts: St. Luc observed from a higher flight of stairs
he saw a resident remove the parasite from Forsythe's mouth and ingest it
other residents were physically touching Forsythe's body in a caressing manner
Forsythe was motionless and surrounded
he reacted with visible distress and fled

memories.memory_text: I was backing away from the pool when someone shoved me hard from behind—I couldn't catch myself, and I fell in, thrashing in the water. Betts came at me fast and pinned me under the surface, holding me down while they all chanted.
grounded_facts: St. Luc was pushed into the pool by Mr. Spergazzi from behind using a cane.
After falling in, St. Luc thrashed in the water.
Betts swam up, grabbed St. Luc, and held him underwater.
Multiple voices, including Betts and other residents, chanted 'A kiss, a kiss, a kiss!'

memories.memory_text: Janine kissed me underwater, but it wasn't human—her throat swelled and something forced its way into my mouth. I felt my own face bulge as tentacles pushed in, and I could taste blood as I struggled before going still.
grounded_facts: Janine surfaced and kissed St. Luc underwater while being assisted by Betts.
During the kiss, Janine's throat and cheeks swelled as a parasite moved from her body into St. Luc's mouth.
St. Luc's cheeks and throat swelled, blood came from his nose and mouth, and he briefly pulled away, seeing tentacles connecting their mouths.
The parasite fully entered St. Luc, causing his body to react with swelling and internal movement before he calmed.

memories.memory_text: I glanced into the rearview mirror and saw the lights of the other cars lined up behind me, all ready to move.
grounded_facts: St. Luc sees the other cars lining up behind his car in the rearview mirror.
The cars have their lights on, indicating they are prepared to follow.

memories.memory_text: I smiled and stepped on the accelerator, feeling the car surge forward as the others began to follow me onto the street.
grounded_facts: St. Luc smiles before stepping on the accelerator.
He accelerates the car, which shoots out into the street.
The other cars begin following his car after he turns onto the street.

memories.memory_text: I was cleaning my teeth with the water-pick, focusing on every detail, and when Janine called about breakfast, I didn’t answer. I just kept going until it felt right.
grounded_facts: Tudor was in the bathroom using a water-pick to clean his teeth with intense focus.
Janine called out from the kitchen that breakfast was almost ready.
Tudor did not respond to her call and continued using the water-pick until he was satisfied.

memories.memory_text: I slid the key into Annabelle's lock, turned it slow, then knocked—just once—before opening the door. I stepped in quiet and shut it behind me, not looking up, just hoping no one saw my face.
grounded_facts: Tudor used a key to unlock Annabelle's apartment door.
He knocked gently before entering.
He entered the apartment and closed the door behind him.
He avoided being seen by others, indicating concern about being recognized.

memories.memory_text: I smelled burned flesh the second I walked in, and I knew—something was wrong, something was so wrong. I saw Hobbes’s foot first, then her on the table, still smoking, eyes open, that clip still on her lips.
grounded_facts: Tudor detects the smell of burned flesh upon entering the apartment.
He sees Hobbes's foot before fully entering the dining room.
He observes Annabelle's corpse on the dining table, still smoking, with surgical clip on her lips and bruises on her neck.
He covers his nose and mouth while approaching.
He winces, blinks rapidly, and moves cautiously to see the corpse's face.

memories.memory_text: I felt it again near my navel—hard, familiar—like it was waiting for me, and I knew right away it hadn't gone away.
grounded_facts: Tudor presses around the area of his navel after a pain spasm
His expression indicates he recognizes the lump
He checks his stomach again after Janine leaves

memories.memory_text: I doubled over silently, the water-pick thrashing in the sink, and I had to hide the pain when Janine looked in—couldn't let her see.
grounded_facts: Tudor doubles over in a soundless spasm of pain
The water-pick writhes in the sink
spraying water
He grabs the water-pick to cover his reaction when Janine appears
He hides twinges while pretending to brush

memories.memory_text: I was on the floor, breathing heavily, and then Mrs. Wheatley was there, helping me up, leaning me on the desk. She used a Kleenex to wipe blood from the corner of my mouth and showed it to me, saying it was from inside me.
grounded_facts: Tudor collapsed behind his desk and was helped to his feet by Mrs. Wheatley
Mrs. Wheatley used a Kleenex to wipe blood from the corner of Tudor's mouth
Tudor was disoriented
breathing heavily
and needed to lean on the desk for support
Mrs. Wheatley pointed out the blood on the Kleenex and said it came from inside Tudor

memories.memory_text: After she left, the pain came again, sharp in my gut, and I felt the blood seep out—I tasted it, metallic, and licked it off my lips without thinking.
grounded_facts: Tudor experienced a subsequent pain twinge after being left alone in his office
Blood trickled from the corner of Tudor's mouth a second time
Tudor licked the blood from his lips with the tip of his tongue

memories.memory_text: I signed the chit, hands shaking, and stepped out of the cab—legs wobbling, like I might fall.
grounded_facts: Tudor signs a chit for the taxi driver.
Tudor exits the taxi.
Tudor is described as unsteady during this scene.

memories.memory_text: I walked in, and the doorman opened the doors, said 'Afternoon, Mr. Tudor'—I didn't look at him, just kept moving, but I felt it, the way he still called me 'Mr.' like I was still someone.
grounded_facts: The doorman opened both doors for Tudor as he entered the building.
The doorman addressed Tudor as 'Mr. Tudor' and greeted him with 'Afternoon, Mr. Tudor.'
Tudor did not respond or make eye contact, consistent with his avoidant and physically reactive traits.

memories.memory_text: The doctor kept staring at my file, moving papers around, like he didn’t believe me. I could feel it—the doubt in the air, heavy, like it was pressing on my chest.
grounded_facts: Dr. St. Luc expressed skepticism about the rapid onset of Tudor's reported symptoms while reviewing his medical file.
St. Luc lingered on the file after Janine left, indicating concern or suspicion.
Tudor was not physically present in the scene, but the discussion about his condition occurred in his absence, which aligns with his avoidant and withdrawn traits.

memories.memory_text: I was on the sofa, just trying to sit still, and then my body locked up—I couldn’t breathe, couldn’t speak—my hands flew to my mouth like something was going to come up, and I fell to the floor, twisting, unable to stop it.
grounded_facts: Tudor enters apartment and attempts to relax on sofa
After brief calm
he suddenly contracts into fetal position
He spills drink
rolls to floor
exhibits extreme physical distress
His mouth opens and closes involuntarily
neck tendons bulge
He struggles back to feet with hands clamped over mouth

memories.memory_text: I barely made it to the bathroom, threw myself over the tub, and gagged—something thick and wet tore out of me, streaked with blood—and then I just collapsed, spent, on the floor, my mouth tasting of iron.
grounded_facts: Tudor stumbles into bathroom with hands over mouth
He kneels on bath mat
head in tub
He gags and vomits blood-streaked slime into tub
He collapses on bathroom floor
mouth bloody
Vomit leaves trail in drain

memories.memory_text: I stumbled into the living room, my breath ragged, and sat facing the glass door—couldn't see straight, just kept gasping, muttering to myself like it would help. Then I got up, opened the door, and stepped onto the balcony, the cold air hitting my skin.
grounded_facts: Tudor enters the living room from the bathroom while wiping his mouth.
He sits on a chair facing the sliding glass door to the balcony.
He is breathing heavily and gasping for air.
He has a dazed expression and mumbles incoherently.
After sitting, he rises, opens the glass door, and steps onto the balcony.

memories.memory_text: I was holding the railing, trying to breathe, and then my neck locked up—my mouth wrenched open, I couldn't stop it, my hands flew to my face but I couldn't hold it down, and I just started retching over the edge, gagging, moaning, I couldn't control any of it.
grounded_facts: Tudor is on a narrow concrete balcony at night.
He is gripping the railing while breathing heavily.
His neck muscles tense suddenly.
His mouth gapes open involuntarily due to muscular tension.
He attempts to suppress vomiting by putting his hands to his mouth.
He ultimately vomits while hanging over the balcony railing.

memories.memory_text: I tapped on my skin near the navel, whispering, 'Here, boy, here,' trying to coax it out again, and when I finally stroked the lump, it pulsed under my fingers like it was answering me.
grounded_facts: Tudor speaks to the lump in his abdomen as if it were a sentient being. He uses tactile stimulation—tapping
scratching
and stroking—to interact with the lump. The lump responds by returning and pulsing under his skin after contact. He expresses approval and connection through repeated phrases like 'Attaboy.'

memories.memory_text: When Janine came in and tried to pull the covers off, I rolled away, hiding it—my stomach—and told her to go away, even though I saw her start to cry.
grounded_facts: Janine enters the bedroom and attempts to see Tudor's abdomen. Tudor actively conceals the lumps by pulling the covers over and rolling away. He rejects her concern with the words 'Go away. Leave me alone.' Janine reacts emotionally
showing distress before leaving the room.

memories.memory_text: My name was called when Forsythe handed over files to St. Luc. She said, 'Files on Horsefield, Tudor, Swinburne, and Velakofsky,' and threw the papers onto the desk.
grounded_facts: Forsythe verbally listed Tudor's name along with others when delivering files to St. Luc.
The files were part of a physical batch of papers and records thrown in groups onto St. Luc's desk.
Tudor's file was included in materials related to patient records and medical publications.

memories.memory_text: They’re meeting at my place, I heard St. Luc say it—apartment 1009, South Tower. I didn’t say yes, I didn’t agree, but they’re coming anyway.
grounded_facts: St. Luc mentions meeting Rollo at Tudor's apartment.
The apartment is specified as 1009 in the South Tower of Starliner Towers.

memories.memory_text: I feel the lumps moving under my skin near my navel, shifting and pulsing, and my hands are pressed there like I'm holding something sacred inside me.
grounded_facts: Tudor is lying on his back with hands on his abdomen around the navel area.
Three lumps are visibly shifting beneath his skin in that region.
His posture resembles that of a pregnant woman, which he mirrors in his hand placement.

memories.memory_text: I'm making these soft, crooning sounds without meaning to, like a lullaby gone wrong, while the things inside me keep moving.
grounded_facts: Tudor makes delirious crooning sounds as the lumps move beneath his skin.
The sounds are described as a parody of a lullaby, produced involuntarily.

memories.memory_text: I called out to Janine from the bed, my voice high and urgent, and when she came, I told her I felt wonderful—so wonderful—and I pulled her close, needing her to start, to begin, because I couldn’t remember how.
grounded_facts: Tudor called Janine repeatedly from the bedroom until she woke up.
He told her 'I feel wonderful' and asked her to sit beside him.
He unbuttoned his shirt while asking her to make love, saying he had forgotten how to start.
He used repetitive, pleading language: 'You will make love to me, won't you, Janine? Won't you make love to me?'

memories.memory_text: She touched my stomach and jerked her hand back like I was something broken—something wrong—and I didn’t understand, I only knew she had to keep going, had to love me, because she was my wife, she was my wife.
grounded_facts: Janine caressed Tudor and then pulled her hand away after feeling lumps on his abdomen.
Tudor reacted with confusion and distress when the contact stopped.
He repeated 'You're my wife, Janine. Please make love to me' in a pleading tone.
He did not acknowledge the lumps directly but focused on the need for continued intimacy.

memories.memory_text: I wailed for Janine, calling her name over and over, my voice tearing out of me like something not mine.

inhuman wail (
Ooooooooooooooooo!") from the bedroom. He then cries out "Janine
Janine
Janinnnnnneeee!
in a wailing tone
prompting Janine to respond. The vocalizations are described as heart-rending and occur while he is on the bed
physically distressed.

memories.memory_text: I felt my throat swelling, pulsing, and my lips twitching like I couldn't control them—something was moving inside me, forcing its way up.
grounded_facts: Tudor's lips move spasmically and silently
described as twitching in an abnormal
insect-like manner. There is a visible
rhythmic swelling in his throat that expands and contracts
suggesting internal movement. These physical changes occur while he is lying on the bed and observed by Janine as she approaches.

memories.memory_text: I heard the knock, and then silence—another knock. I stayed still. Then the knob turned, and the door opened. I didn’t say come in. I didn’t say anything.
grounded_facts: Rollo knocked on Tudor's door twice.
Tudor did not respond to the knocks.
Rollo entered the apartment without permission after checking the door number.
Tudor was present inside the apartment during the intrusion.

memories.memory_text: I was lying in bed, so weak, couldn't move or speak—just floating in and out, feeling the weight of my body but not in control. I heard a voice calling my name, but I couldn't answer, couldn't even close my mouth.
grounded_facts: Tudor was found lying stiffly on the bed, mouth agape, eyes shut, unresponsive.
He did not move or respond when Rollo entered and called out to him.
The scene implies a state of physical collapse or unconsciousness.

memories.memory_text: I felt the covers pulled back, cold air on my skin, and then—something moved inside me. I didn't see it, but I knew they were there, my blood, my life—I had to protect them. I couldn't let him hurt them.
grounded_facts: Rollo draws back the covers, exposing Tudor's abdomen.
A blood parasite emerges from Tudor's body.
Tudor later exhibits delusional attachment to the parasites, referring to them as part of his blood and self.
